hero.title: Full Stack Developer — Every problem has a solution.
hero.title_alt: Full Stack Developer — I think in problems, I build solutions.
hero.subtitle: Juan Manuel Vila

childhood.title: I always wanted to know how things work.
childhood.subtitle: The origin
childhood.text_1: I grew up in Chivilcoy, Argentina, always getting into my dad's work — repairs, air conditioning installations, whatever he was doing. From an early age I was drawn to understanding how things worked and how to handle them with my hands. That curiosity never left.
childhood.text_2: I started electronic engineering, and in my third year I had my first programming class in C. Something clicked. I realized that programming had everything I loved about engineering — solving real problems — but with a huge advantage: you can test, fail, and fix in real time.
childhood.text_3: That led me to change my major and enroll in a University Programmer Analyst program—seeking a more formal foundation for everything I was already learning on my own. In these times of increased productivity, analyst skills are increasingly important, and that training gave me a broader perspective on how to design effective solutions, not just code that works.

abstract.title: Practice teaches what theory cannot.
abstract.subtitle: Background & today
abstract.text_1: Since I started programming I've been learning through personal projects, mistakes, and an obsession with understanding things deeply. University gave me the structural foundation, but the hands-on experience I built myself — and today I feel ready to tackle diverse problems that a degree alone doesn't prepare you for.
abstract.text_2: Lately I've been exploring how to automate Gaussian Splatting generation, and I'm shipping a production app for technicians, workshops and service professionals — publishing on the Play Store for the first time, targeting a sector with real and growing demand. Along the way I've built up experience across the full JavaScript/TypeScript ecosystem — React, Next.js, React Native with Expo — alongside Python, Django, Flask, Rust and tools like Power BI.
abstract.text_3: I firmly believe a good developer never stops learning. Technology evolves constantly, and keeping up isn't just a professional necessity — it's what makes this work genuinely exciting.

contributions.title: Consistency over immediacy.
contributions.subtitle: Code history
contributions.text_1: Coding every day is a habit I built over time — not out of obligation, but because there's always something to learn, improve, or explore. This graph reflects that.
contributions.text_2: Throughout my career, I built approximately five complex projects working in teams using agile methodologies, Scrum, Python, Rust, Node.js, NestJS, Next.js, Django, and Flask. These periods took a backseat to my personal projects, but they helped me understand how teams work and the different methodologies for solving various problems.
